MS. HENRY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND PUT IN SUCH EVIDENCE THAT SHOWS TO THE TRIABLE ISSUES IN CONFORMITY WITH CIVIL CODE 437C(G). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MENLO CAPITAL GROUP, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF MENLO CAPITAL GROUP, LLC IS DENIED IN PART AND GRANTED IN PART. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE FOLLOWING CLAIMS: 1) BREACH OF CONTRACT 2) THE FRAUD CAUSE OF ACTION AND 4) VIOLATION OF BUSINESS AND PROFESSION CODE SETION 17200 BECAUSE THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO CLAIM 3) BREACH OF THE IMPLIED COVENANT AS AN ISSUE OF LAW THERE IS NO IMPLIED COVENANT. THE COURT IS NOT ADDRESSING CLAIM 5) DUTY UNDER THE ALTER EGO DOCTRINE AS IT IS UNNECESSARY AND IT DOES NOT RESOLVE A CAUSE OF ACTION UNDER THE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION STATUTE. MS. HENRY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND PUT IN SUCH EVIDENCE THAT SHOWS TO THE TRIABLE ISSUES IN CONFORMITY WITH CIVIL CODE 437C(G). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION To The First And Second Causes Of Action Of The Cross-Complaint Of 851 California, Llc
Off Calendar Apr-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Suzanne Henry of Newhouse Law Group PC telephone number 310-684-3162 for Plaintiff Lindsey Scully.
Brandon Braga of Manning & Kass telephone number 213-624-6900 for Cross-Defendant Lindsey Scully.
Charles Schaible and Patrick Gunn of Cooley LLP telephone number 415-693-2101 for Defendants Menlo Capital Group LLC and Sunil Suri.

This matter was uncontested. The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT LINDSEY SCULLY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION To The First And Second Causes Of Action Of The Cross-Complaint Of 851 California, LLC is denied as to the first cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty and the request for punitive damages and granted as to the second cause of action for breach of contract. The Salata declaration shows that the motion was served in a timely fashion. However, even if it was not, cross-complainant 851 California, LLC (851) responded on the merits and thus waived its timeliness objection. (Carlton v. Quint, (2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 690, 697.) There is a triable issue whether plaintiff breached her fiduciary duty by making misrepresentations to 851 and caused damage to 851 which can be recovered based on the tort of another doctrine. Specifically, there are triable issues whether: 1) Scully falsely represented that Nelson Brothers were not looking to raise capital and could consummate the deal; 2) 851 relied on Scully's representations and Nelson Brother's representation in the sale agreement that it could complete the sale, or a combination of both; and 3) 851 was damaged by its reliance on the asserted misrepresentations by incurring attorney's fees. As to the breach of contract claim, 851 fails to present competent evidence demonstrating any contractual damages. Karan Suri's testimony lacks foundation and is speculation about how Scully's conduct caused the property to diminish in value. (Suri dec., pars. 21-22; See Jones v. Wachovia Bank (2014) 230 Cal.App.4th 935, 950-951.) Moreover, at deposition pages 186-187 and 261-262, Karan Suri admitted that he had no opinion regarding whether the property decreased in value as a result of plaintiff's conduct. Scully's objections to Karan Suri's declaration as contradicting his deposition testimony and lacking foundation are sustained. 851 acknowledges that San Francisco property values have significantly appreciated since 2011. (UMF 41). 851's contention that it suffered "lost opportunity" damages lacks merit. These alleged damages are based on an inadmissible stream of conjecture. Scully's objections to the cited portions of paragraph 20 of the Karan Suri's declaration are sustained. The motion is denied as to 851's request for punitive damages because there is a triable issue whether Scully acted fraudulently. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion Of Menlo Net Lease Holdings, Llc For A Protective Order
Off Calendar Mar-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES:

DEFENDANT MENLO NET LEASE HOLDINGS, LLC. 's Motion For A Protective Order is scheduled this day. There being no an appearance nor an objection the Court adopts its Tentative Ruling.

Motion for a Protective Order is denied. It is the plaintiff's statutory right to depose defendant's PMK pursuant to CCP section 2025.230. The agreement of counsel at the deposition of Akhil Suri his testimony was on his own behalf and also as PMK for Menlo Lease Holdings was not unambiguous. There was discussion and reference of the possibility of questions and topics to which the witness could not respond because they had not been specified in a formal notice. The PMK deposition statute requires a deponent to testify as to the extent information is known or reasonably available. There were multiple topics about which the witness was not informed at that time which were reasonably available. A formal PMK notice has been served identifying specific areas of inquiry and requiring the production of documents. The plaintiff is entitled to discovery of the topics specified. The deposition of the PMK shall take place on or before April 22, 2016 or such other date as the parties shall agree. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Court signed Order in open court.

Judge Pro-tem: Steven Stein; Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Motion For Relief Under Code Of Civil Procedure §473(B) Regarding Plaintiffs Motions To Compel Further Responses From All Menlo Defendants To Plaintiffs First Set Of Form And Special Interrogatories, Document Requests And Requests For Admission
Off Calendar Oct-13-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFF LINDSEY SCULLY'S Motion For Relief Under Code Of Civil Procedure Sec. 473(B) Regarding Plaintiff's Motions To Compel Further Responses From All Menlo Defendants To Plaintiffs First Set Of Form And Special Interrogatories, Document Requests And Requests For Admission, the court adopts the recommendation of Judge Pro Tem Sigrid Irias' modified as follows:
"Motion for relief under CCP 473(b) is denied due to lack of a showing justifying discretionary relief under CCP 473(b), regardless of whether missing the 45-day deadline for making a motion to compel might in some circumstances qualify for relief under the statute. This is not a case where mandatory relief is implicated. (1) Missing the 45 day deadline for a motion to compel does not involve a "default judgment" or "dismissal." (2) Rather than an attorney declaration of fault, plaintiff's counsel attempts to blame a floater secretary at plaintiff's counsel's firm for the failure to meet the deadline. There is a complete failure to accept responsibility for the decision to file 15 motions to compel on the same day, for the decision to wait until the very last day to try to file them and/ or for the decision not to engage in further meet and confer after defense counsel offered to provide some supplemental responses. There is no explanation of how it came to be that the secretary received the papers at the last second on April 17, giving the secretary only two hours, at most, before the 6:30 p.m. Fed Ex cutoff to print 35 documents, make copies, sort them into stacks for service, and get them to Fed Ex. Tactical decisions (such as the decision to file 15 motions to compel rather than engage in further meet and confer or to request an extension for the motion deadline), are not eligible for either discretionary or mandatory relief under Section 473." Prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Charles M. Schaible, Esq. of Cooley LLP, T:415.693.2101, for defendants and moving party;
Suzanne M. Henry, Esq. of Buchalter Nemer, T:213.630.5621, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court modified its tentative ruling only as to the third cause of action of DEFENDANTs MENLO CAPITAL GROUP, LLC ET AL, MENLO REALTY INCOME PROPERTIES 27, LLC, MENLO REALTY INCOME PROPERTIES 28, LLC, SUNIL SURI, REALTY INCOME CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer as to the First Cause of Action for breach of contract is sustained in part and overruled in part. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to inclusion of the proper parties to the contract under the alter ego doctrine. Plaintiff must allege, in good faith, "sufficient facts to show a unity of interest and ownership, and an unjust result if the corporation is treated as the sole actor." Leek v. Cooper (2011) 194 Cal.App.4th 399, 415. The demurrer as to the First Cause of Action is overruled as to whether the language of the Agreement encompasses the named parties and whether breach has occurred. The demurrer as to the Second Cause of Action for Unjust Enrichment should be sustained in part and overruled in part. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to Defendant Menlo Capital Group LLC because it is undisputed that Defendant Menlo Capital Group LLC is a party to the Agreement and the Plaintiff cannot pursue a breach of contract cause of action and unjust enrichment cause of action simultaneously. Wal-Noon Corp. v. Hill, (1975) 45 Cal.App.3d 605, 613. The demurrer is overruled as to Defendants Sunil Suri, Menlo Realty Income Properties 27, LLC, Menlo Realty Income Properties 28, LLC, and Realty Income Corporation because the language of the Agreement may or may not encompass these defendants and the Plaintiff is allowed to allege multiple and conflicting theories of recovery. Flournoy v. State, (1969) 275 Cal.App.2d 806, 811. The demurrer as to the Third Cause of Action for Fraud is sustained WITH leave to amend under the economic loss rule because it is not conceptually distinct from the Agreement. See United Guaranty Mortgage Indemnity Co. v. Countrywide Fin. Corp. (C.D. Cal. 2009) 660 F.Supp2d 1163, 1184. The demurrer as to the Fourth Cause of Action for breach of the implied covenant is overruled. The demurrer as to the Sixth Cause of Action for violations of Business & Professions Code 17200 et seq. is overruled. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR# 5976
